paper_title: Material and Methods of Pollen Wasp Study in Anti Atlas, Morocco

methods.description: This method involves the observation and documentation of pollen wasp behavior and the collection of plant specimens in the Anti Atlas region of Morocco.

steps.description: Investigations were carried out on four days between 13 and 17 April 2017 at two localities in the Anti Atlas in southern Morocco.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct field investigations at specified locations and dates

steps.description: Geographic coordinates (WGS 84) were measured using a Garmin GPS 12.
Dry specimens of Quartinia major from both localities were identified using the key by Richards (1962) and compared with the holotype at the Natural History Museum Vienna.
steps.searchable_summary: identify and compare specimens with holotype

steps.description: Observations were made with a close-up binocular and documented by using a Canon EOS 70D camera with a macro lens and macro flash-lights.
steps.searchable_summary: document observations using binoculars and camera

steps.description: Specimens of all plant species that were visited by pollen wasps were collected and preserved dried.
The plant taxa were identified following Fennane et al. (1999, 2007); the nomenclature follows the African Plant Database (version 3.4.0).
steps.searchable_summary: identify plant taxa using specified references

methods.description: This method involves observing and analyzing the behavior of pollen wasps at flowers and nests.

steps.description: Flower preferences of imagines were studied by counting the number of sightings of flower visiting individuals.
Sightings were counted while walking randomly along flowering plants or during unsystematic, random observations.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct random transect and unsystematic observations

steps.description: Female behavior at the nest was observed for approximately 180 min.
steps.searchable_summary: observe female wasp behavior at nest

methods.description: This method involves preparing and analyzing pollen samples from brood cell provisions.

steps.description: Pollen samples from two brood cell provisions were prepared using the method outlined by Westrich and Schmidt (1986).
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pollen samples using specified method

steps.description: The different pollen types were identified under a light microscope at magnifications of times 400 or 1000.
steps.searchable_summary: identify pollen types using light microscope

methods.description: This method involves the excavation and analysis of wasp nests.

steps.description: Nests were marked in the field with little ice-cream national flags.
Nests were excavated using a combination of two reading glasses for magnification.
Nest dimensions were measured using a calliper rule.
Cells along with their contents were stored in vials and investigated under a stereomicroscope.
